fr.elements: Main Building; Front Driveway; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the visibility and accessibility of the property entrance could significantly support emergency responder access. Clearing or thinning the dense vegetation near the designated access point and along primary response routes may improve navigation and movement through the property, allowing responders to identify critical areas quickly. Establishing and maintaining clearly marked pathways along the driveway and ensuring the vehicle parking area remains unobstructed could facilitate staging and maneuvering space for emergency vehicles. Additionally, implementing reflective markers or lighting at key points might further assist responders during nighttime operations or in low visibility conditions, thereby ensuring an efficient and effective emergency response.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Scattered Shrubs; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetated Access Route; Structure Surrounded by Vegetation; Tree Cluster; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Brush Concentration
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property by increasing the separation between scattered trees and shrubs can reduce overall fuel continuity and improve the property's preparedness. Managing the vegetated perimeter and reducing the concentration of brush and other vegetation near the structure could further decrease potential structure exposure. Creating clear, open spaces around the structure and maintaining these areas would contribute to improved defensible space. The existing vegetated access route may hinder firefighter access, so it would be beneficial to manage vegetation along this route to support potential suppression operations. Addressing vegetation adjacent to the structure and spacing tree clusters more effectively may also limit the continuity of combustible materials and support long-term resilience against wildfires while improving overall vegetation management and safety.
